'''Pierre Chamorin''' is a French former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who represented [[France national rugby league team|France]] at the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup|1995 World Cup]].

==Playing career==
Chamorin started his career for [[AS Saint-Estève]], with which he won several [[French Rugby League Championship|French Championship]] and [[Lord Derby Cup]] titles in the 1990s.
He made his debut for [[France national rugby league team|France]] in 1989 against [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]]. He went on to play in seventeen test matches for France, including against the [[1994 Kangaroo tour of Great Britain and France|1994 Kangaroos]]. His last match was at the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup|1995 World Cup]], against [[Samoa national rugby league team|Western Samoa]].<ref name=rlp/>
Chamorin played in [[Super League I]] and [[Super League II]] for [[Paris Saint-Germain Rugby League|Paris Saint-Germain]], captaining the side.<ref>[https://archive.today/20130125160603/http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1P2-4800366.html Paris will miss Russell] ''The Independent'', 13 July 1996</ref><ref>[https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/chamorin-has-heart-to-stir-paris-romance-1344705.html Chamorin has heart to stir Paris romance] ''The Independent'', 29 March 1996</ref>

==Personal life==
His father is [[Henri Chamorin]], a former rugby league international who played as second row or as lock forward in the 1960s.<ref name="Mouret December 2011 64">{{Cite book|last=Mouret|first=Aimé|title=Le Who's who du rugby à XIII|publisher=Éditions de l'Ixcea|date=December 2011|isbn=978-2-84918-118-8|location=Toulouse|pages=64}}</ref>

After his playing career, he "is in charge of the training of the juniors".<ref name="Mouret December 2011 64"/>

==Honours==

* [[French Rugby League Championship]]:
**Champion in 1989, 1990, 1993 and 1997 ([[AS Saint-Estève|Saint-Estève]])
** Runner-up in 1992, 1995 and 1996 (Saint-Estève)
*[[Lord Derby Cup]]
**Champion in 1994, 1995, and 1998 (Saint-Estève)
** Runner-up in 1990 (Saint-Estève)
